response:
you can solve this problem by using prefix sums to calculate the values quickly for each index k. since the array is sorted you only need to track the sum of elements before and after the current position to find the absolute differences or squared differences. the twist requires that every third index uses a square calculation instead of the standard absolute difference logic.

***
the algorithm runs in linear time which makes it efficient for large inputs where k represents the index. you must ensure that your code handles the squared difference rule correctly when the index is a multiple of three. this approach avoids nested loops and keeps the complexity low while satisfying all the constraints given in the problem statement.
